jokes.text: Hey, Perez, didn't I just tell you a couple of days ago that your father died? What? Oh, Perez, I meant to, uh... Oh...
jokes.setup: Louie tries to demonstrate his experience delivering death news
jokes.punchline: He accidentally tells Perez his father died while trying to give an example
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Louie's insensitivity creates an accidentally devastating reveal. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: Look, take the rest of the day off... Look, Perez, the Lord works in mysterious ways.
jokes.setup: Louie has just accidentally told Perez his father died
jokes.punchline: Louie awkwardly tries to comfort him with religious platitudes
jokes.explanation: Extends the cringe moment effectively but less original than the initial reveal.

jokes.text: He never was. He lives in Boston.
jokes.setup: Louie says Jim's father 'is no longer with us'
jokes.punchline: Jim misinterprets it literally - dad was never physically present because he lives in Boston
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim misunderstanding - perfectly in character and economically delivered.

jokes.text: A condo in Palm Beach?
jokes.setup: Louie says dad has gone to his 'final resting place'
jokes.punchline: Jim thinks it's a retirement community
jokes.explanation: Another logical Jim misinterpretation, though slightly less surprising than the first.

jokes.text: Boy, how ironic. That's how much Dad weighed.
jokes.setup: Jim is assigned cab 410 after learning of his father's death
jokes.punchline: He notes the 'irony' that the cab number matches his father's weight
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that perfectly captures Jim's unique processing of grief - morbid but delivered with innocent wonder.

jokes.text: Garcia? You told Perez.
jokes.setup: Louie mentions Garcia's dad also died this week
jokes.punchline: Alex realizes Louie can't even remember the name of the guy whose father he accidentally killed
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that compounds Louie's insensitivity - he doesn't even know the victim's name.

jokes.text: Ah, no!
jokes.setup: Louie realizes he can't even remember which employee's father died
jokes.punchline: His horrified reaction to his own incompetence
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that caps the running gag about Louie's insensitivity effectively.

jokes.text: The Yanks lost another one? Would I be this upset just 'cause the Yanks lost another game? Double-header? To Seattle.
jokes.setup: Tony seems devastated about something
jokes.punchline: Escalating reveals it's increasingly embarrassing Yankees losses
jokes.explanation: Good character beat for Tony's sports obsession but fairly predictable structure.

jokes.text: The domino theory. You see, Steinbrenner ruins the spirits of the Yanks, they lose. Their fans become depressed and irritable... Before you know it, OPEC is raising its prices, and there's another war breaking out in South America.
jokes.setup: Tony claims Steinbrenner is changing the world
jokes.punchline: Elaborate conspiracy theory connecting Yankees losses to global chaos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and perfectly Tony - takes his sports obsession to cosmic proportions with internal logic.

jokes.text: I just heard the Russians launched another killer satellite. Steinbrenner.
jokes.setup: Tony's domino theory about Steinbrenner affecting world events
jokes.punchline: Blames even Soviet space program on Yankees owner
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with maximum economy - single word punchline that lands the absurdity.

jokes.text: A cake donut?! Oh. Jeff! This man is an attorney. Give me that box. Eight years of school. Here. A maple log.
jokes.setup: Louie wants to impress the lawyer
jokes.punchline: Frantically upgrades from cake donut to maple log based on education level
jokes.explanation: Great Louie moment - his social climbing through pastry selection is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, this is my lucky day.
jokes.setup: Lawyer gets upgraded donut treatment from clearly sketchy taxi dispatcher
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic appreciation of the 'luxury' treatment
jokes.explanation: Functional but unremarkable - standard 'fish out of water' reaction.

jokes.text: Who are you? I-I... I'm John Bickers, y-your father's attorney? Oh.
jokes.setup: Lawyer assumes Jim will recognize him
jokes.punchline: Jim has no idea who he is despite the formal introduction
jokes.explanation: Typical Jim obliviousness but not particularly clever or surprising.

jokes.text: My father dropped me out of that when I dropped out of Harvard.
jokes.setup: Discussion of father's will
jokes.punchline: Jim says he was 'dropped out' of the will when he 'dropped out' of Harvard
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that sounds like Jim's innocent language patterns while being accidentally clever.

jokes.text: Three and a half million dollars.
jokes.setup: Building to reveal of inheritance amount
jokes.punchline: Massive sum delivered with dramatic pause
jokes.explanation: More plot than joke but the dramatic beat and everyone's reaction creates comedy.

jokes.text: I beg your pardon. I couldn't help overhearing.
jokes.setup: Louie was obviously eavesdropping on inheritance news
jokes.punchline: Pretends he accidentally overheard $3.5 million announcement
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie greed moment - transparent but in character.

jokes.text: Bless them.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explains Jim's siblings are trying to prove him incompetent to steal his money
jokes.punchline: Jim's genuinely positive reaction to what should be devastating news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim response - his guileless nature turns what should be outrage into blessing. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: That sounds pretty good.
jokes.setup: Lawyer explains Jim would be treated like a child under conservatorship
jokes.punchline: Jim thinks being treated like a child sounds appealing
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Jim moment - his childlike nature makes the threat sound appealing to him.

jokes.text: I haven't had many fights in my life, but what I've had, I've won. I got my driver's license. I got my throat checked without a tongue depressor.
jokes.setup: Jim declares he's ready to fight for his inheritance
jokes.punchline: His 'victories' are completely mundane administrative tasks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim logic - treats basic life tasks as heroic victories. Great escalation from license to throat exam.

jokes.text: Or fail to do so!
jokes.setup: Jim's passionate declaration about fighting for his inheritance
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts himself by acknowledging he might fail
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of his own heroic moment - maximum economy and purely Jim's self-awareness.

jokes.text: Uh, competent.
jokes.setup: Judge asks how Jim is feeling today
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact response in a competency hearing
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - the literal answer to the implied question with perfect economy.

jokes.text: This man is so far ahead of the rest of us that most people often mistake his wisdom for burnt-out brain cells.
jokes.setup: Alex defending Jim's intelligence
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges Jim appears brain-damaged while claiming he's actually wise
jokes.explanation: Clever way to acknowledge the obvious while maintaining defense - good structure and memorable phrasing.

jokes.text: Boss, if you feel like that about me, who cares about the money? Shut up, you idiot!
jokes.setup: Jim is moved by Louie's defense of his intelligence
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately calls him an idiot, undermining his own argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Louie's greed overrides his defense strategy. Great ironic structure.

jokes.text: You know, it's one of the problems in this court that they tend to rerun Mr. Deeds Goes to Town as often as they do.
jokes.setup: Alex gives impassioned defense of Jim's decency
jokes.punchline: Judge notes this exact scenario happens regularly in movies/TV
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-joke acknowledging the familiar courtroom drama trope while staying in character.

jokes.text: What about George Steinbrenner? Is a man competent that would let Reggie Jackson go to the Angels? That would go through four managers in one year?
jokes.setup: Tony brings up Howard Hughes as example of rich eccentric
jokes.punchline: Pivots to Steinbrenner incompetence using specific baseball failures
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Tony's Steinbrenner obsession but the execution is a bit forced.

jokes.text: Mr. Steinbrenner isn't the subject of this hearing... unfortunately.
jokes.setup: Tony rants about Steinbrenner in court
jokes.punchline: Judge's dry aside that he'd like to rule on Steinbrenner's competence
jokes.explanation: Good judicial deadpan - suggests even the judge has opinions about Yankees management.

jokes.text: That's as comfortable as I can get.
jokes.setup: Judge asks if Jim wants to stand or sit comfortably
jokes.punchline: Jim indicates his current awkward position is optimal
jokes.explanation: Implies Jim is in some odd physical position - typical character beat but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Nothing. Except that I wanted to invite everybody here to my victory party at the Pierre Hotel. We're going to have plenty of those big shrimp.
jokes.setup: Judge asks what Jim wanted to say
jokes.punchline: Jim assumes he's won and invites everyone to celebration with fancy details
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jim's optimism and specific detail about shrimp adds personality.

jokes.text: No, no. No, a decorator friend helped me.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks if Jim picked out his apartment furniture
jokes.punchline: Jim claims professional help for what's presumably awful decor
jokes.explanation: Implies visual gag about bad apartment but relies on assumption about what we're seeing.

jokes.text: I didn't know I graduated.
jokes.setup: Jim finds his graduation picture in father's trunk
jokes.punchline: Jim is surprised to learn he actually graduated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - somehow unaware of his own graduation. Maximum economy and purely character-driven.

jokes.text: Wait! I'm crying. But am I crying because I lost you or because I didn't cry? Wait, I stopped. But I was crying. I didn't want to stop. I'm crying again. No, I stopped.
jokes.setup: Jim feels bad about not crying for his father
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on his own crying while actively crying and stopping
jokes.explanation: Touching but funny - Jim's self-awareness about grief creates awkward comedy in emotional moment.

jokes.text: You like Stevie Wonder?
jokes.setup: Jim discovers cassette tape his father left him
jokes.punchline: Jim's surprise that his father liked Stevie Wonder
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment - Jim's surprise at father's musical taste. More touching than funny.

jokes.text: Good night, Mr. Walters!
jokes.setup: Jim has been listening to emotional Stevie Wonder song
jokes.punchline: Jim cheerfully says goodnight to someone off-screen after emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim - transitions from emotional moment to mundane social interaction without missing beat.
